questions.q: In which field of Indian cinema is Mrinal Sen (1923-2018) best remembered?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen was one of India's foremost film directors, known for his politically engaged cinema.

questions.q: Which 1969 Hindi film by Mrinal Sen is widely credited with helping launch the Indian New Wave?
questions.explanation: Bhuvan Shome (1969) is often cited as a film that helped trigger the Indian New Wave movement.

questions.q: In which Indian state was filmmaker Mrinal Sen primarily based throughout his career?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen worked mainly out of Kolkata in West Bengal and made many of his films in Bengali.

questions.q: In which year was the celebrated Indian filmmaker Mrinal Sen born?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen was born on 14 May 1923 in Faridpur, in present-day Bangladesh.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen is most often grouped alongside which two other directors as one of the great masters of Bengali parallel cinema?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen, Satyajit Ray and Ritwik Ghatak are regarded as the triumvirate of Bengali art cinema.

questions.q: In which language was the majority of Mrinal Sen's filmography made?
questions.explanation: Although he made films in Hindi, Telugu and Oriya, most of Mrinal Sen's work was in Bengali.

questions.q: The city that features prominently in Mrinal Sen's 'Calcutta trilogy' of the early 1970s is named in the trilogy's title. Which city is it?
questions.explanation: The Calcutta trilogy consists of Interview, Calcutta 71 and Padatik, all set in Calcutta.

questions.q: Which actress played the female lead in Mrinal Sen's 1969 film Bhuvan Shome?
questions.explanation: Suhasini Mulay made her debut as the village girl Gauri in Bhuvan Shome.

questions.q: India's highest honour in cinema, the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, was conferred on Mrinal Sen in which year?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2005 for his lifetime contribution to Indian cinema.

questions.q: In which year did the noted Indian filmmaker Mrinal Sen pass away?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen died on 30 December 2018 in Kolkata at the age of 95.

questions.q: Which 1980 Mrinal Sen film, whose title translates to 'In Search of Famine', deals with a film crew shooting a movie about the Bengal famine?
questions.explanation: Akaler Sandhane (1980) follows a film unit making a movie about the 1943 Bengal famine in a village.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1976 Hindi film Mrigayaa marked the feature film debut of which future superstar?
questions.explanation: Mithun Chakraborty made his film debut in Mrigayaa (1976), winning the National Award for Best Actor.

questions.q: Which three films make up Mrinal Sen's 'Calcutta trilogy'?
options: Bhuvan Shome, Mrigayaa and Oka Oori Katha
Ek Din Pratidin, Kharij and Akaler Sandhane
questions.explanation: The Calcutta trilogy comprises Interview (1971), Calcutta 71 (1972) and Padatik (1973).

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1977 Telugu film Oka Oori Katha was based on a story by which celebrated Hindi-Urdu writer?
questions.explanation: Oka Oori Katha (1977) was adapted from Munshi Premchand's story 'Kafan'.

questions.q: Which 1984 Mrinal Sen film, whose title means 'The Ruins', won him acclaim and starred Shabana Azmi and Naseeruddin Shah?
questions.explanation: Khandhar (1984) starred Shabana Azmi and Naseeruddin Shah and was based on a Premendra Mitra story, 'Telenapota Abishkar'.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1982 film Kharij ('The Case Is Closed') examines the death of a young domestic servant. At which festival did it win the Jury Prize?
questions.explanation: Kharij (1982) won the Jury Prize (Prix du Jury) at the 1983 Cannes Film Festival.

questions.q: Before establishing himself as a filmmaker, Mrinal Sen took a job for a pharmaceutical company in which capacity?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen worked for a time as a medical representative for a pharmaceutical company before pursuing cinema.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1980 film Ek Din Pratidin ('And Quiet Rolls the Dawn') centres on a family thrown into crisis by what event?
options: A working daughter failing to return home one night
questions.explanation: Ek Din Pratidin (1980) follows a middle-class family's anxiety when their earning daughter does not come home one evening.

questions.q: Late in his life Mrinal Sen served as a nominated Member of which house of the Indian Parliament?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen was a nominated Member of the Rajya Sabha, the upper house of India's Parliament.

questions.q: Which veteran actor played the title role of the upright railway officer Bhuvan Shome in Mrinal Sen's 1969 film of that name?
questions.explanation: Utpal Dutt played the stern bureaucrat Bhuvan Shome whose outlook is softened by a village girl.

questions.q: The narration in Mrinal Sen's Bhuvan Shome was voiced by which young actor who later became a major Bollywood star?
questions.explanation: A young Amitabh Bachchan provided the voice-over narration for Bhuvan Shome (1969).

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's first feature film, released in 1955, shared its release year with Satyajit Ray's Pather Panchali. What was its title?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen's directorial debut was Raat Bhore (1955), which he himself considered a weak first effort.

questions.q: Which 1959 Mrinal Sen film, telling the story of a Chinese hawker in 1930s Calcutta, became the first film to be banned in independent India?
questions.explanation: Neel Akasher Neechey (1959) followed a Chinese street vendor in 1930s Calcutta and was briefly banned by the Indian government.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1991 film Mahaprithivi ('World Within, World Without') reflected on the impact of which contemporary global event?
options: The fall of the Berlin Wall and collapse of communism
The end of apartheid in South Africa
questions.explanation: Mahaprithivi (1991) responded to the fall of the Berlin Wall and the crumbling of the communist bloc.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1986 film Genesis was an international co-production filmed largely in the desert of which Indian state?
questions.explanation: Genesis (1986) was shot in the Rajasthan desert and featured Shabana Azmi, Naseeruddin Shah and Om Puri.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1971 film Interview, which opens his Calcutta trilogy, follows a young man's frustrating quest to obtain what?
options: A room to rent in the city
A Western-style suit for a job interview
questions.explanation: In Interview (1971), the protagonist scrambles to find a Western suit needed for a job interview, exposing class and colonial pretensions.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen published a volume of memoirs reflecting on his life and cinema. What is the title of this English-language autobiography?
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen's memoir, 'Always Being Born', reflects on his life, his city Calcutta and his cinema.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's 1989 film Ek Din Achanak ('Suddenly, One Day') revolves around what central mystery?
options: A professor who walks out one day and vanishes without a trace
questions.explanation: Ek Din Achanak (1989) follows a family coping with the sudden, unexplained disappearance of its retired-professor patriarch.

questions.q: Mrinal Sen's final feature film, released in 2002 when he was nearly eighty, was titled what?
questions.explanation: Aamaar Bhuvan (2002) was Mrinal Sen's last feature film, set in a rural Muslim community in West Bengal.

questions.q: France honoured Mrinal Sen for his contribution to cinema by appointing him to which order?
options: Ordre des Arts et des Lettres (Commander)
questions.explanation: Mrinal Sen was made a Commander of the Ordre des Arts et des Lettres by the French government.
